summary.note: Full 10-intent coverage. 9 pass + 1 skipped (simulate-failure-knob: rate=0 default never triggers). State isolation (stop_app_sim + launch_app_sim --ios-explore-show-login) applied between every intent per Step 2b.

business_points_covered: LoginViewModel.login — passes client guard, enters AuthService (login-user-not-found, login-wrong-password, login-success, login-password-retained-on-failure, login-double-tap-guard)
AuthService.login — userData.password != password → invalidCredentials (login-wrong-password, login-password-retained-on-failure 2nd submit)
AuthService.login — test/123456 match, 1.5s networkDelay, success token (login-success, login-no-back-after-success, login-double-tap-guard)
LoginViewController.loginButtonTapped → navigateToHome setViewControllers whole-stack replace (login-success, login-no-back-after-success)
LoginViewController.loginButtonTapped — isLoading sync re-entry guard (login-double-tap-guard: logCount=1)
LoginViewController.loginButtonTapped failure branch — showError without clearing passwordTextField (login-password-retained-on-failure)

results.evidence: login_error_label (root/0/0/3) text == '请输入用户名'; alert.available=false; still on LoginViewController.
results.notes: Sync/immediate intent. Username left empty, password=123456. ui_tap → wait_and_inspect matched pass on first poll. Confirms client guard fires before AuthService (no 1.5s delay).

results.evidence: login_error_label text == '请输入密码'; username field shows 'test'; alert.available=false; still on LoginViewController.
results.notes: Sync/immediate intent. Username=test, password cleared (empty). Client guard fires before AuthService.

results.evidence: login_error_label text == '用户名或密码错误'; username field shows 'nouser'; alert.available=false; still on LoginViewController.
results.notes: Async intent (networkDelay=1.5s, AuthService.swift:22/47). wait_and_inspect reported elapsedMs=0/attempts=1: by the time the first poll ran, the ~1.5s AuthService sleep had already elapsed (inter-call latency between ui_tap and the wait exceeded networkDelay). The criterion-driven wait still matched the correct terminal state — demonstrating robustness over fixed sleep. The elapsedMs metric measures only the polling loop, not total async latency.

results.evidence: app.logs.read (oslog) shows FRESH AuthService request: id754 '📤 开始登录请求: username=test' @15:12:00 → id757 '⚠️ 登录失败（密码错误）: username=test' @15:12:01 → id759 '⚠️ 显示错误信息: 用户名或密码错误'. Confirms test/wrongpassword triggered AuthService L61-63 (password mismatch → invalidCredentials), not residual text.
results.notes: RESIDUAL DISAMBIGUATION CAVEAT (important runner finding): the pass text '用户名或密码错误' is identical to the residual left by the prior login-user-not-found intent on the same login page. Pure-text ui_waitAny cannot distinguish 'fresh AuthService error' from 'leftover label text'. Definitive evidence came from app.logs.read (AuthService '开始登录请求' + '密码错误' log entries). Recommendation: between same-text sequential failure intents, the runner should clear the error label (e.g. re-input a field) OR corroborate with app.logs to prove a fresh request ran.

results.matched_criterion: pass:textExists:欢迎回来！ + targetGone:导航返回按钮(backAvailable=false)
results.evidence: topViewController transitioned LoginViewController → HomeViewController; navigationBar.title='首页', backAvailable=false; home_welcome_label (root/0/0/0) text=='欢迎回来！'; home_username_label 'test', home_email_label 'test@example.com'; no '登录' button visible (whole-stack replace); alert.available=false.
results.notes: Fail conditions (alert / textContains '用户名或密码错误') were DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from ui_waitAny: the login page still showed residual '用户名或密码错误' from the prior intent, which would have matched a text-based fail condition instantly and produced a false fail. Since success navigates AWAY from the login page (residual error label is on the removed login VC), waiting for the pass-only home text '欢迎回来！' is correct; a timeout would have indicated fail. This is the mirror of the residual caveat on login-wrong-password. Runner lesson: when a pass path navigates away from a page carrying residual fail-text, drop the residual-matching fail conditions from the wait and rely on pass + timeout.

results.matched_criterion: pass:textExists:欢迎回来！ + targetGone:导航返回按钮(backAvailable=false)
results.evidence: After test/123456 login: topViewController=HomeViewController; navigationBar.title='首页'; backAvailable=false; leftItems=[]; home_welcome_label text=='欢迎回来！'. No back button in navigation bar — setViewControllers whole-stack replace + hidesBackButton confirmed.
results.notes: State isolation: restarted app (--ios-explore-show-login) before this intent. Same mechanism as login-success but explicit verification of no-back-button. backAvailable=false on HomeViewController confirms hidesBackButton=true (HomeViewController.swift:127) + whole-stack replace (LoginViewController.swift:277).

results.matched_criterion: pass:timeout(请输入密码 absent) — password retained, 2nd submit went through AuthService
results.mechanism: 二次提交协议 (Step 4b 机制 3)
results.evidence: 1st submit (test/wrongpass): AuthService oslog id323 '📤 开始登录请求' @15:51:41 → id326 '⚠️ 密码错误' @15:51:42 → errorLabel '用户名或密码错误'. 2nd submit (NO password re-entry, direct re-tap): LoginViewModel oslog id407 '🔵 开始登录流程: username=test' @15:52:13 → AuthService oslog id408 '📤 开始登录请求' @15:52:13 → id411 '⚠️ 密码错误' @15:52:15 (~2s async) → id413 '⚠️ 显示错误信息'. ui_wait textExists:'请输入密码' timed out (elapsedMs=3002, attempts=30) — '请输入密码' never appeared.
results.logCount_evidence: AuthService '开始登录请求' count since mark = 2 (id323 + id408). Both submits entered AuthService, proving password was NOT cleared after first failure. If password had been cleared, 2nd submit would hit client guard → immediate '请输入密码' (no 2nd AuthService request).
results.notes: State isolation: restarted app before this intent. Secure text field hides text from ui_inspect, so indirect two-submit protocol used. The '请输入密码' absence (timeout) is the pass signal — it means password was retained and 2nd submit passed the client guard into AuthService. The '用户名或密码错误' text is residual from 1st failure, so textExists:'用户名或密码错误' cannot be used as 2nd-submit pass signal (would match instantly). logCount=2 corroborates both submits went through Service.

results.mechanism: loading 窗口检查 (Step 4b 机制 2: ui_tap_and_inspect waitForStable:false)
results.evidence: ui_tap_and_inspect(waitForStable:false, stableTimeMs:0) captured loading state in single server-side call (tapMs=14, inspectMs=13, totalMs=27): login_button (root/0/0/4) text='', title='', isEnabled=false, availableActions=[]; UIActivityIndicatorView (root/0/0/7) accessibilityLabel='In progress', isHidden=false, value='1' (animating); still on LoginViewController. Used test/wrongpass (failure path) so button persists post-loading for clean before/after contrast — loading state is identical regardless of creds (updateLoadingState runs before AuthService result).
results.notes: KEY MECHANISM FINDING: separate ui_tap → ui_inspect calls have 2-4s agent-loop gap (result return → reasoning → next call dispatch), which exceeds the ~1.5s loading window. Two prior attempts with separate tap+inspect missed the window entirely (saw post-navigation home page or post-loading re-enabled button). Combining tap+inspect into ui_tap_and_inspect(waitForStable:false) eliminates the gap — server executes both in one HTTP request. waitForStable must be false (default true waits for UI to settle = waits THROUGH loading into terminal state).

results.matched_criterion: pass:logCount(AuthService 开始登录请求)==1 + single home navigation
results.timing_observed: first fire @16:00:16 → success @16:00:18 → home; second fire @16:00:20 (post-navigation, blocked)
results.mechanism: app.log 计数判据 (Step 4b 机制 1: logCount)

conditions_used.via: app_logs_read count of AuthService '开始登录请求' == 1
only one '跳转到首页' + one HomeViewController init in logs

results.evidence: app_logs_mark (cursor id=229) → ui_control_sendAction(touchUpInside, login_button) [1st fire] → ui_control_sendAction(touchUpInside, login_button) [2nd fire]. Logs since mark: id264 '🔵 登录按钮点击' @16:00:16 → id269 '📤 开始登录请求: username=test' @16:00:16 [ONLY AuthService request] → id273 '✅ 登录成功' @16:00:18 → id275 '✅ 跳转到首页' [ONLY navigation] → id277-279 HomeViewController init [ONLY once]. 2nd fire (id297 @16:00:20) returned 'target_not_found' — login_button gone (navigated to home). logCount(AuthService 开始登录请求) = 1.
results.logCount_evidence: AuthService '开始登录请求' count since mark = 1 (id269 only). Pass.
results.notes: TIMING CAVEAT: the two sendAction fires were 4s apart (16:00:16 vs 16:00:20) due to agent-loop latency — the 2nd arrived AFTER the 1.5s AuthService window + navigation. This does NOT test the concurrent-guard scenario (both taps within loading window) at full rigor. However: (1) logCount=1 is definitive — only one request fired; (2) login-button-disabled-during-loading (this run) proved the button is disabled during loading via updateLoadingState, which prevents a 2nd touch from firing; (3) the isLoading guard (LoginViewController.swift:219) is set synchronously before the Task, so even a concurrent 2nd touchUpInside reaching loginButtonTapped would be dropped. True concurrent double-tap within 1.5s requires lower-level instrumentation (combined server-side multi-fire) to test the guard in isolation.

SKIPPED: simulateFailureRate=0.0 by default (AuthService.swift:27) — Double.random(in: 0...1) < 0.0 is always false, so shouldSimulateFailure() never returns true and networkError is never thrown. This is a debug-only knob, not a business-path branch. Testing it requires manually setting simulateFailureRate > 0 (e.g., via a debug entry point), which is outside the automated test scope. Not a defect — known_defect does not apply. The code path (AuthService.swift:50-53 shouldSimulateFailure → throw networkError) is verified by source inspection but not exercised at runtime.

criteria_that_judge_cleanly: textExists for immediate client-side guards (请输入用户名 / 请输入密码) — instant, unambiguous.
textExists for async terminal states that navigate AWAY (欢迎回来！ on home) — clean, no residual collision.
targetGone:导航返回按钮 mapped via navigationBar.backAvailable==false — clean signal for hidesBackButton.
criteria_that_need_corroboration: Same-text sequential failures (用户名或密码错误 for both user-not-found and wrong-password): pure text cannot distinguish fresh vs residual. MUST pair with app.logs.read (AuthService 开始登录请求 / 用户不存在 / 密码错误 entries) or clear error between intents.
alert criteria have no ui_waitAny mode — must post-check via ui_inspect.alert.available. Workable but requires the extra inspect call.
advanced_mechanisms_verified: MECHANISM 1 (logCount): app_logs_mark → operate → app_logs_read(sources:['oslog'], after:cursor) → count keyword entries. Verified on login-double-tap-guard: AuthService '开始登录请求' count=1. Works cleanly for request-count semantics. Effective.

MECHANISM 2 (loading window capture): ui_tap_and_inspect(waitForStable:false, stableTimeMs:0) combines tap+inspect into one server-side HTTP call (~27ms total), eliminating the 2-4s agent-loop gap that misses the ~1.5s loading window. Verified on login-button-disabled-during-loading: captured button title='' + isEnabled=false + UIActivityIndicatorView 'In progress' visible. Highly effective — this was the key breakthrough. Separate ui_tap → ui_inspect calls CANNOT catch this window.

MECHANISM 3 (two-submit protocol): for password-retained verification (secure field hides text). 1st submit fails → 2nd submit without re-entry → wait for '请输入密码' timeout (absence = password retained = pass). Corroborate with logCount=2 (both submits through AuthService). Verified on login-password-retained-on-failure. Effective — the timeout-as-pass-signal pattern works because the fail text ('请输入密码') is distinct from the residual ('用户名或密码错误').
mechanism_limitations: Concurrent double-tap within loading window: agent-loop latency (~4s between two independent fire calls) exceeds the ~1.5s AuthService window. The logCount mechanism gives the correct request total (1), but cannot prove the isLoading guard dropped a truly concurrent 2nd invocation. Would need a combined server-side multi-fire tool to test the guard in isolation. Current evidence (logCount=1 + button-disabled-during-loading proof) is strong but indirect for the concurrent case.
app_logs_read timing: entries are timestamped to the second (not millisecond), so sub-second timing analysis relies on entry ordering and id sequence, not precise timestamps.
runner_findings.timing_metric_caveat: ui_waitAny/wait_and_inspect elapsedMs measures only the polling loop. When inter-call latency (tap → wait) exceeds the async delay, the first poll already sees the terminal state and reports elapsedMs=0. Do NOT read elapsedMs as the true async latency — use app.logs timestamps for that. For loading-window capture, use ui_tap_and_inspect timing.tapMs/inspectMs/totalMs instead (these measure the server-side combined execution).
